title: Seattle 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: Seattle 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Seattle, United States: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Space Needle and Chihuly Garden and Glass. Prices from $2439 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Seattle, United States — Temperate marine climate with mild, gray, wet winters (highs around 8°C, frequent drizzle) and pleasantly dry, mild summers (highs 22-24°C, rarely humid). June to September offers the driest, sunniest weather with long daylight hours; late September into October brings fall colors with thinner crowds. This itinerary blends Space Needle, Chihuly Garden and Glass, Pike Place Market with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Space Needle, Chihuly Garden and Glass, Pike Place Market
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Seattle
itinerary.description: Land at Seattle-Tacoma International Airport (SEA) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Space Needle — Iconic 1962 World's Fair tower with rotating glass floor observation deck. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Seattle: Chihuly Garden and Glass & Museum of Pop Culture (MoPOP)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Chihuly Garden and Glass — Dale Chihuly blown-glass art installations and garden. In the afternoon, continue to Museum of Pop Culture (MoPOP) — Frank Gehry-designed music, sci-fi and pop culture museum. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Seattle: Pacific Science Center & Seattle Aquarium
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Pacific Science Center — Hands-on science exhibits, IMAX and planetarium. In the afternoon, continue to Seattle Aquarium — Puget Sound marine life on the downtown waterfront. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Mount Rainier National Park
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Mount Rainier National Park, a popular side trip from Seattle. Return to Seattle in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Mount Rainier National Park

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Ballard Locks fish ladder window — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Seattle: Woodland Park Zoo & Bill Speidel's Underground Tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Woodland Park Zoo — Large zoo known for naturalistic habitat exhibits. In the afternoon, continue to Bill Speidel's Underground Tour — Guided walk through Pioneer Square's buried 19th-century streets. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Snoqualmie Falls
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Snoqualmie Falls, a popular side trip from Seattle. Return to Seattle in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Seattle: Argosy Cruises Harbor Tour & Museum of Flight
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Argosy Cruises Harbor Tour — Narrated boat tour of Elliott Bay and the working port. In the afternoon, continue to Museum of Flight — One of the world's largest air and space museums, near Boeing Field. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Discovery Park West Point Lighthouse — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Bainbridge Island ferry trip
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Bainbridge Island ferry trip, a popular side trip from Seattle. Return to Seattle in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Bainbridge Island ferry trip

itinerary.title: Seattle: Seattle Great Wheel & Seattle Art Museum (SAM)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Seattle Great Wheel — 175-foot Ferris wheel on Pier 57. In the afternoon, continue to Seattle Art Museum (SAM) — Downtown fine art museum with Northwest Coast Native art collection. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Seattle: Pike Place Market & Kerry Park
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Pike Place Market — Historic public market famous for fish-throwing fishmongers and local vendors. In the afternoon, continue to Kerry Park — Best-known skyline viewpoint with Space Needle and Mount Rainier backdrop. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Volunteer Park Conservatory — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Gas Works Park or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to SEA for your departure flight.

travelTips: Buy a Seattle CityPASS if visiting the Space Needle, aquarium and MoPOP to save on combined entry.
Visit Pike Place Market before 9am to see vendors setting up without the midday crowds.
Book Chihuly Garden and Glass and Space Needle combo tickets online in advance, especially in summer.
A Mount Rainier day trip needs an early start — the Paradise parking area fills by mid-morning in summer.
Skip the tourist trap "original Starbucks" line and try a local roaster like Victrola or Espresso Vivace instead.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
A waterproof shell jacket rather than an umbrella (locals rarely use umbrellas)
Layers for a day that can swing from drizzle to sun
A warm layer even in summer evenings near the water
thingsToAvoid: Jaywalking downtown — Seattle police do issue tickets, unlike many US cities.
Driving on I-5 or SR-520 during weekday rush hour (7-9am, 4-6:30pm) — traffic backs up severely.
Leaving valuables visible in parked cars near Pike Place or Pioneer Square — car break-ins are common.
Assuming it always rains — summer is genuinely dry, so pack accordingly rather than over-packing rain gear.
Walking Pioneer Square and parts of downtown alone late at night — stick to well-lit, busier streets.
topRestaurants: The Walrus and the Carpenter — Oyster Bar ($$$)
Salumi Artisan Cured Meats — Italian Deli ($)
Matt's in the Market — Seasonal Seafood ($$$)
adventureActivities: Hiking the Discovery Park loop trail
Whale watching day trips from Edmonds
luxuryExperiences: Spa day at Four Seasons Seattle
Seaplane scenic flight with Kenmore Air
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset seaplane flight over Puget Sound
Private wine tasting in Woodinville wine country
Weekend escape to a Snoqualmie Falls lodge
budgetExperiences: Free wander through Pike Place Market
Hiking free trails in Discovery Park

festivalCalendar.name: Seafair
festivalCalendar.timing: July to early August, culminating in the Hydroplane races

localEtiquette: Tipping 18-20% at restaurants and bars is expected and often defaults on card machines.
Locals dress casually even at nicer restaurants — "Seattle formal" is jeans with a nice top.
Jaywalking is actually enforced downtown; use crosswalks even when the street looks empty.
Coffee culture runs deep — ordering a drip coffee at a local roaster rather than a chain is the norm.
Small talk with strangers is less common than in the South; the "Seattle Freeze" politeness-but-distance is a real local trait.
localTransportGuide: Link light rail runs directly from Sea-Tac Airport to downtown, Capitol Hill and the University District — load an ORCA card for buses, light rail, the South Lake Union Streetcar and Washington State Ferries. Seattle is hilly and spread across water, so ride-shares and ferries fill the gaps transit doesn't reach.
bestTimeToVisit: June to September offers the driest, sunniest weather with long daylight hours; late September into October brings fall colors with thinner crowds.
weather: Temperate marine climate with mild, gray, wet winters (highs around 8°C, frequent drizzle) and pleasantly dry, mild summers (highs 22-24°C, rarely humid).
visaInformation: Visa-free ESTA entry for UK, EU, Australian, Japanese and other Visa Waiver Program passport holders for stays up to 90 days. Indian nationals and most other nationalities need a US B1/B2 visitor visa arranged in advance through a US embassy or consulate interview.
averageFlightTime: ~14.2h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Seattle?
faqs.answer: Visa-free ESTA entry for UK, EU, Australian, Japanese and other Visa Waiver Program passport holders for stays up to 90 days. Indian nationals and most other nationalities need a US B1/B2 visitor visa arranged in advance through a US embassy or consulate interview.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Seattle?
faqs.answer: June to September offers the driest, sunniest weather with long daylight hours; late September into October brings fall colors with thinner crowds. Temperate marine climate with mild, gray, wet winters (highs around 8°C, frequent drizzle) and pleasantly dry, mild summers (highs 22-24°C, rarely humid).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Seattle safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Seattle is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Seattle?
faqs.answer: The local currency is USD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Sofia Sharma
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Kerry Park in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Sarah Garcia
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Seattle is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Seattle Aquarium. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Chen Smith
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Olympic Sculpture Park in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Olivia Cohen
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Our trip to Seattle was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Seattle Great Wheel was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Ethan Dubois
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Pike Place Market and Chihuly Garden and Glass. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Yuki Smith
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Gas Works Park alone was worth the trip, but combined with Argosy Cruises Harbor Tour this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Yuki Ivanov
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Chihuly Garden and Glass alone was worth the trip, but combined with Alki Beach this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Grace Ivanov
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Waterfront Park and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
